Name: Choate Rosemary Hall
Tagline: An independent boarding and day school in Wallingford, CT for talented students in grades 9 through postgraduate. Students discover and define their unique selves with endless opportunities for explo
About: Choate Rosemary Hall, a co-educational boarding and day school for students grades 9-PG, is tucked in the scenic New England town of Wallingford, Connecticut. Open to learning 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, our 458 acres, state-of-the-art facilities, and dedication to exceptionally transformative experiences offer students a path to discovery uniquely tailored to their talents and interests.
Philosophy: Choate attracts intellectually gifted and motivated students from diverse backgrounds whose commitment to serious study is enhanced in this personally supportive and academically challenging setting. On a campus that inspires a particular sensitivity to beauty, teachers—who share genuine respect and affection for young people—impart an enthusiasm for life and for learning.
Outcomes: Choate Rosemary Hall boasts a strong track record of Ivy League placements and high AP exam scores. Notable alumni include John F. Kennedy and Adlai Stevenson, underscoring the school's commitment to developing influential leaders.
Admissions: Anthony's Pizzeria and Deli, 368 Main Street, (203)626-9435, experience a fun, re-creation of the pizza and deli cuisine, using Italian heritage to replicate classic recipes and applying a modern-day twist. Prospective families are encouraged to visit campus as part of their admission process.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: The KEC hosted a susTainABLE gathering on Sun Day to celebrate renewable energy. Many thanks to Janusz for preparing a wonderful meal, which included vegetables from our garden.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The KEC Mission is to create a scholarly community dedicated to promoting environmental understanding, stewardship of the land, and social responsibility.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The Kohler Environmental Center (KEC) is Choate’s leading-edge environmental research and education center located just east of the main campus.
Performance.Academic Integration: Choate Rosemary Hall offers a robust curriculum with over 300 courses, including 30 AP classes and unique electives like Environmental Immersion. Signature programs like the Science Research Program foster hands-on, interdisciplinary learning.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates from Choate Rosemary Hall have an impressive 33% acceptance rate to Ivy League schools, with notable alumni such as John F. Kennedy and Adlai Stevenson. The school consistently ranks high in national academic competitions.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Choate Rosemary Hall maintains quality through NYSAIS accreditation and utilizes ERB assessments for tracking student progress. Regular trimester reports and parent-teacher conferences ensure comprehensive feedback and continuous improvement.
